message:
feat(fnp-python): nested submodules — linalg/ma/testing/lib.recfunctions (br-franken_numpy-k74v.5)

Resolves k74v.5 (fnp_python submodule namespace strategy question) by
implementing option (A)/(B) hybrid: real nested PyModule submodules
that expose numpy-native spellings via alias from the already-registered
flat functions.

Before: np.linalg.svd(a) failed under fnp_python; users had to write
fnp_python.svd(a) or fnp_python.linalg_svd(a).

After:
  import fnp_python as np
  np.linalg.svd(a)           # resolves through m.linalg.svd alias
  np.ma.count(m)             # resolves through m.ma.count alias
  np.testing.assert_allclose # resolves through m.testing alias
  np.lib.recfunctions.drop_fields(...)  # two-level nested alias

Submodules wired up at the end of #[pymodule] fn fnp_python:
- linalg: 28 members (3 linalg_* + 25 top-level aliases)
- ma:     29 members (5 ma_* + 24 top-level aliases)
- testing: 8 members (all testing_* aliased to their unprefixed names)
- lib.recfunctions: 5 members (two-level nested)

The flat prefixed names (linalg_eig, ma_count, testing_assert_allclose,
recfunctions_drop_fields, etc.) REMAIN registered at the top level for
backwards compatibility — nothing breaks for existing callers.

Added k74v_5_nested_submodules_expose_numpy_native_names test that
asserts every expected attribute resolves through the nested namespace
and that fnp_python.linalg.svd returns the same numpy.linalg result
type as numpy.linalg.svd on np.eye(3).

Workspace: 395 fnp-python lib tests passing under parallel cargo test.
